Mergen
  • How It Works
  • Capabilities

  • Pricing
  • Install
  • GitHub
Get Started
Install

Choose Your
Method

Multiple ways to install Mergen depending on your preferences and environment. Start with Method 1 for the smoothest experience.

🚀
01

NPM

Easiest — Recommended

Best for: Quick setup, always latest version

Time~2 minutes
One command setup
npx mergen-server@latest setup
Or install globally
npm install -g mergen-server
mergen-server setup
🐳
02

Docker

Zero Dependencies

Best for: Containerised environments, consistent deployments

Time~1 minute
Pull and run
docker run -p 3000:3000 mergen/server:latest
Or with docker-compose
curl -O https://raw.githubusercontent.com/omertt27/Mergen/main/docker-compose.yml
docker-compose up
🍺
03

Homebrew

macOS

Best for: Mac users who prefer native packages

Time~3 minutes
Add tap and install
brew tap omertt27/mergen
brew install mergen
Run setup
mergen-server setup
📦
04

Pre-Built Binary

No Node.js Required

Best for: No Node.js, offline environments

Time~2 minutes

Download for your OS

macOS (Apple Silicon)macOS (Intel)LinuxWindows
Make executable (Mac/Linux)
chmod +x mergen-macos-arm64
Run setup
./mergen-macos-arm64 setup
🔧
05

From Source

Developers

Best for: Contributors, local development

Time~5 minutes
Clone and build
git clone https://github.com/omertt27/Mergen.git
cd Mergen/server
npm install
npm run build
Run setup
node ../scripts/setup.mjs
🌐
06

One-Line Installer

Quick Automated Setup

Best for: Quick automated setup

Time~2 minutes
Run in terminal
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/omertt27/Mergen/main/install.sh | bash
Browser Extension

After Installing
the Server

🏪

Chrome Web Store

Recommended

Add to Chrome
🔧

Manual Install

Developer mode required

  1. Open chrome://extensions
  2. Enable Developer mode (top right)
  3. Click Load unpacked
  4. Select the extension/ folder
Verify

Check Your
Installation

Run after any install method
mergen-server --version
mergen-server test
mergen-server start
Expected output
✓ Server binary exists
✓ Server starts successfully
✓ Health endpoint responds
✓ Event ingestion works
✓ IDE configured correctly
✨ Mergen is ready to use!
Compare

Methods at
a Glance

MethodTimeDependenciesUpdatesBest For
NPM2 minNode.jsAutoMost users
Docker1 minDockerManualContainers
Homebrew3 minNonebrew upgradeMac users
Binary2 minNoneManual downloadNo Node.js
Source5 minNode.js, Gitgit pullDevelopers
One-liner2 mincurl + bashRe-run scriptAutomation
Troubleshooting

Common
Issues

"Command not found: mergen-server"
npx mergen-server@latest setup
./mergen-macos-arm64 setup  # binary path
"Port 3000 already in use"
lsof -ti:3000 | xargs kill -9
"Server not responding"
curl http://127.0.0.1:3000/health
mergen-server start
"IDE not showing Mergen tools"
# Restart your IDE after setup
mergen-server test
# Cursor:    ~/.cursor/mcp.json
# VS Code:   ~/.vscode/mcp.json
# Windsurf:  ~/.codeium/windsurf/mcp_config.json
Next Steps

You're Ready.
Start Debugging.

01
Start server
mergen-server start
02
Open your AI IDE
Cursor / Claude Code / VS Code
03
Ask your AI
"Get recent logs"
04
Web UI (optional)
http://127.0.0.1:3000/setup
GitHubReport an Issue

Security

✓ No data leaves your machine✓ Server runs on localhost only (127.0.0.1)✓ Open source (MIT license)✓ No cloud services, no analytics
